377:   def calculate_dominant_color!(local_path = nil)
378:     color = nil
379: 
380:     color = "" if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}") || extension == "svg"
381: 
382:     if color.nil?
383:       local_path ||=
384:         if local?
385:           Discourse.store.path_for(self)
386:         else
387:           Discourse.store.download(self)
388:         end
389: 
390:       if local_path.nil?
391:         # Download failed. Could be too large to download, or file could be missing in s3
392:         color = ""
393:       end
394: 
395:       color ||=
396:         begin
397:           data =
398:             Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
399:               "nice",
400:               "-n",
401:               "10",
402:               "convert",
403:               local_path,
404:               "-depth",
405:               "8",
406:               "-resize",
407:               "1x1",
408:               "-define",
409:               "histogram:unique-colors=true",
410:               "-format",
411:               "%c",
412:               "histogram:info:",
413:               timeout: DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
414:             )
415: 
416:           # Output format:
417:           # 1: (110.873,116.226,93.8821) #6F745E srgb(43.4798%,45.5789%,36.8165%)
418: 
419:           color = data[/#([0-9A-F]{6})/, 1]
420: 
421:           raise "Calculated dominant color but unable to parse output:\n#{data}" if color.nil?
422: 
423:           color
424:         rescue Discourse::Utils::CommandError
425:           # Timeout or unable to parse image
426:           # This can happen due to bad user input - ignore and save
427:           # an empty string to prevent re-evaluation
428:           ""
429:         end
430:     end
431: 
432:     if persisted?
433:       self.update_column(:dominant_color, color)
434:     else
435:       self.dominant_color = color
436:     end
437:   end

633:               post.cooked.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)

635:                 Post.with_deleted.where(id: post.id).update_all(raw: post.raw, cooked: post.cooked)
636:               end
637:             end
638: 
639:             upload.optimized_images.find_each(&:destroy!)
640:             upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
641:             # remove the old file (when local)
642:             FileUtils.rm(path, force: true) unless external
643:           rescue => e
644:             problems << { upload: upload, ex: e }
645:           ensure
646:             file&.unlink
647:             file&.close
648:           end
649:         end
650:       end
651:     end
652: 
653:     problems
654:   end
655: 
656:   def self.extract_upload_ids(raw)
657:     return [] if raw.blank?
658: 
659:     sha1s = []
660: 
661:     raw.scan(/\/(\h{40})/).each { |match| sha1s << match[0] }
662: 
663:     raw
664:       .scan(%r{/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)})
665:       .each { |match| sha1s << Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(match[0]) }
666: 
667:     Upload.where(sha1: sha1s.uniq).pluck(:id)
668:   end
669: 
670:   def self.backfill_dominant_colors!(count)
671:     Upload
672:       .where(dominant_color: nil)
673:       .order("id desc")
674:       .first(count)
675:       .each { |upload| upload.calculate_dominant_color! }
676:   end
677: 
678:   private
679: 
680:   def short_url_basename
681:     "#{Upload.base62_sha1(sha1)}#{extension.present? ? ".#{extension}" : ""}"
682:   end

684:   def should_convert_video?
685:     !skip_video_conversion && SiteSetting.video_conversion_enabled &&
686:       SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_uploads && FileHelper.is_supported_video?(original_filename) &&
687:       !OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: id)
688:   end

3: module HasUrl
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern

6:   class_methods do
7:     def extract_url(url)
8:       url.match(self::URL_REGEX)
9:     end
10: 
11:     def extract_sha1(path)
12:       data = extract_url(path)
13:       return if data.blank?
14: 
15:       sha1 = data[2]
16:       return if sha1&.length != Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
17: 
18:       sha1
19:     end
20: 
21:     def get_from_url(url)
22:       return if url.blank?
23: 
24:       uri =
25:         begin
26:           URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
27:         rescue URI::Error
28:         end
29: 
30:       return if uri&.path.blank?
31:       data = extract_url(uri.path)
32: 
33:       if data.blank?
34:         result = nil
35:         result ||= self.find_by(url: uri.path)
36:         return result
37:       end
38: 
39:       result = nil
40: 
41:       if self.name == "Upload"
42:         sha1 = data[2]
43:         result = self.find_by(sha1: sha1) if sha1&.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
44:       end
45: 
46:       result || self.find_by("url LIKE ?", "%#{data[1]}")
47:     end
48: 
49:     def get_from_urls(upload_urls)
50:       urls = []
51:       sha1s = []
52: 
53:       upload_urls.each do |url|
54:         next if url.blank?
55: 
56:         uri =
57:           begin
58:             URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
59:           rescue URI::Error
60:           end
61: 
62:         next if uri&.path.blank?
63:         urls << uri.path
64: 
65:         if data = extract_url(uri.path).presence
66:           urls << data[1]
67:           sha1s << data[2] if self.name == "Upload"
68:         end
69:       end
70: 
71:       self.where(url: urls).or(self.where(sha1: sha1s))
72:     end
73:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   include HasUrl
5:   belongs_to :upload
6: 
7:   # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
8:   VERSION = 2
9:   URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
10: 
11:   def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
12:     @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
13:     # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
14:     # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
15:     #
16:     # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
17:     if lock_per_machine?
18:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
19:         DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
20:       end
21:     else
22:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
23:     end
24:   end
25: 
26:   def self.lock_per_machine?
27:     return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
28:     @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
32:     @lock_per_machine = value
33:   end
34: 
35:   def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
36:     return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
37:     return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
38: 
39:     # no extension so try to guess it
40:     upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
41: 
42:     if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
43:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
44:         raise InvalidAccess
45:       else
46:         # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
47:         return
48:       end
49:     end
50: 
51:     # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
52:     extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
53:     thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
54: 
55:     # correct bad thumbnail if needed
56:     if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
57:       thumbnail.destroy!
58:       thumbnail = nil
59:     end
60: 
61:     return thumbnail if thumbnail
62: 
63:     store = Discourse.store
64: 
65:     # create the thumbnail otherwise
66:     original_path = store.path_for(upload)
67: 
68:     if original_path.blank?
69:       # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
70:       original_path = store.download(upload)
71:     end
72: 
73:     if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
74:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
75:         raise InvalidAccess
76:       else
77:         # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
78:         return
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     lock(upload.id, width, height) do
83:       # may have been generated since we got the lock
84:       thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
85: 
86:       # return the previous thumbnail if any
87:       return thumbnail if thumbnail
88: 
89:       if original_path.blank?
90:         Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
91:       else
92:         # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
93: 
94:         return nil if extension.length == 1
95: 
96:         temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
97:         temp_path = temp_file.path
98: 
99:         target_quality =
100:           upload.target_image_quality(
101:             original_path,
102:             SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
103:           )
104:         opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
105:         opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
106: 
107:         # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
108:         if extension == ".svg"
109:           FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
110:           resized = true
111:         elsif opts[:crop]
112:           resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
113:         else
114:           resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
115:         end
116: 
117:         if resized
118:           # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
119:           thumbnail =
120:             OptimizedImage.create!(
121:               upload_id: upload.id,
122:               sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
123:               extension: extension,
124:               width: width,
125:               height: height,
126:               url: "",
127:               filesize: File.size(temp_path),
128:               version: VERSION,
129:             )
130: 
131:           # store the optimized image and update its url
132:           File.open(temp_path) do |file|
133:             url = store.store_optimized_image(file, thumbnail, nil, secure: upload.secure?)
134:             if url.present?
135:               thumbnail.url = url
136:               thumbnail.save
137:             else
138:               Rails.logger.error(
139:                 "Failed to store optimized image of size #{width}x#{height} from url: #{upload.url}\nTemp image path: #{temp_path}",
140:               )
141:             end
142:           end
143:         end
144: 
145:         # close && remove temp file
146:         temp_file.close!
147:       end
148: 
149:       thumbnail
150:     end
151:   end
152: 
153:   def destroy
154:     OptimizedImage.transaction do
155:       Discourse.store.remove_optimized_image(self) if self.upload
156:       super
157:     end
158:   end
159: 
160:   def local?
161:     !(url =~ %r{\A(https?:)?//})
162:   end
163: 
164:   def calculate_filesize
165:     path =
166:       if local?
167:         Discourse.store.path_for(self)
168:       else
169:         Discourse.store.download!(self)
170:       end
171:     File.size(path)
172:   end
173: 
174:   def filesize
175:     if size = read_attribute(:filesize)
176:       size
177:     else
178:       size = calculate_filesize
179: 
180:       self[:filesize] = size
181:       update_columns(filesize: size) if !new_record?
182:       size
183:     end
184:   end
185: 
186:   def self.safe_path?(path)
187:     # this matches instructions which call #to_s
188:     path = path.to_s
189:     return false if path != File.expand_path(path)
190:     return false if path !~ %r{\A[\w\-\./]+\z}m
191:     true
192:   end
193: 
194:   def self.ensure_safe_paths!(*paths)
195:     paths.each { |path| raise Discourse::InvalidAccess unless safe_path?(path) }
196:   end
197: 
198:   IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
199: 
200:   def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
201:     opts ||= {}
202: 
203:     # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
204:     # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
205:     # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
206:     # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
207:     # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
208:     extension = nil
209:     if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
210:       extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
211:     else
212:       extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
213:     end
214: 
215:     if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
216:       raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
217:     end
218:     "#{extension}:#{path}"
219:   end
220: 
221:   def self.thumbnail_or_resize
222:     SiteSetting.strip_image_metadata ? "thumbnail" : "resize"
223:   end
224: 
225:   def self.resize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
226:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
227: 
228:     # note FROM my not be named correctly
229:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
230:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
231: 
232:     instructions = ["convert", "#{from}[0]"]
233: 
234:     instructions << "-colors" << opts[:colors].to_s if opts[:colors]
235: 
236:     instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
237: 
238:     # NOTE: ORDER is important!
239:     instructions.concat(
240:       %W[
241:         -auto-orient
242:         -gravity
243:         center
244:         -background
245:         transparent
246:         -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
247:         #{dimensions}^
248:         -extent
249:         #{dimensions}
250:         -interpolate
251:         catrom
252:         -unsharp
253:         2x0.5+0.7+0
254:         -interlace
255:         none
256:         -profile
257:         #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
258:         #{to}
259:       ],
260:     )
261:   end
262: 
263:   def self.crop_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
264:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
265: 
266:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
267:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
268: 
269:     instructions = %W{
270:       convert
271:       #{from}[0]
272:       -auto-orient
273:       -gravity
274:       north
275:       -background
276:       transparent
277:       -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
278:       #{dimensions}^
279:       -crop
280:       #{dimensions}+0+0
281:       -unsharp
282:       2x0.5+0.7+0
283:       -interlace
284:       none
285:       -profile
286:       #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
287:     }
288: 
289:     instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
290: 
291:     instructions << to
292:   end
293: 
294:   def self.downsize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
295:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
296: 
297:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
298:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
299: 
300:     %W{
301:       convert
302:       #{from}[0]
303:       -auto-orient
304:       -gravity
305:       center
306:       -background
307:       transparent
308:       -interlace
309:       none
310:       -resize
311:       #{dimensions}
312:       -profile
313:       #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
314:       #{to}
315:     }
316:   end
317: 
318:   def self.resize(from, to, width, height, opts = {})
319:     optimize("resize", from, to, "#{width}x#{height}", opts)
320:   end
321: 
322:   def self.crop(from, to, width, height, opts = {})
323:     optimize("crop", from, to, "#{width}x#{height}", opts)
324:   end
325: 
326:   def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
327:     optimize("downsize", from, to, dimensions, opts)
328:   end
329: 
330:   def self.optimize(operation, from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
331:     method_name = "#{operation}_instructions"
332: 
333:     instructions = self.public_send(method_name.to_sym, from, to, dimensions, opts)
334:     convert_with(instructions, to, opts)
335:   end
336: 
337:   MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE = 500_000
338:   MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS = 20
339: 
340:   def self.convert_with(instructions, to, opts = {})
341:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
342:       "nice",
343:       "-n",
344:       "10",
345:       *instructions,
346:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS,
347:     )
348: 
349:     allow_pngquant = to.downcase.ends_with?(".png") && File.size(to) < MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE
350:     FileHelper.optimize_image!(to, allow_pngquant: allow_pngquant)
351:     true
352:   rescue => e
353:     if opts[:raise_on_error]
354:       raise e
355:     else
356:       error = +"Failed to optimize image:"
357: 
358:       if e.message =~ /\Aconvert:([^`]+)/
359:         error << $1
360:       else
361:         error << " unknown reason"
362:       end
363: 
364:       Discourse.warn(
365:         error,
366:         upload_id: opts[:upload_id],
367:         location: to,
368:         error_message: e.message,
369:         instructions: instructions,
370:       )
371:       false
372:     end
373:   end
374: end

440:     def self.content_disposition_for(filename, disposition: nil)
441:       disposition ||= FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename) ? "inline" : "attachment"
442:       return "" if filename.blank?
443: 
444:       header = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename:)
445:       return header if header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
446: 
447:       extension = File.extname(filename)
448:       basename = File.basename(filename, extension)
449: 
450:       # Binary search for the longest basename that fits within the limit
451:       low = 1
452:       high = basename.length
453: 
454:       while low < high
455:         mid = (low + high + 1) / 2
456:         candidate = "#{basename[0...mid]}#{extension}"
457:         test_header =
458:           ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename: candidate)
459:         if test_header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
460:           low = mid

463:         end
464:       end

3: module Jobs
4:   class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 1.hour
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
9: 
10:       # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
11:       Upload
12:         .by_users
13:         .where(
14:           "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
15:         )
16:         .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
17:         .where(url: "")
18:         .find_each(&:destroy!)
19: 
20:       return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
21: 
22:       # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
23:       last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
24:       if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
25:            (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
26:         return
27:       end
28: 
29:       result = Upload.by_users
30:       Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
31: 
32:       # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
33:       # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
34:       # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
35:       # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
36:       # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
37:       result =
38:         result
39:           .where(
40:             "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",

43:           .where(
44:             "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
45:           )
46:           .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
47:           .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
48:           .with_no_non_post_relations
49: 
50:       result.find_each do |upload|
51:         next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
52:         upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
53:       end

338:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)

347:     begin
348:       execute_convert(from, to, opts)

359:   def convert_to_jpeg!
360:     return if @opts[:for_site_setting]
361:     return if filesize < MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
362: 
363:     jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
364: 
365:     from = @file.path
366:     to = jpeg_tempfile.path
367: 
368:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)

370:     from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
371:     to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)

373:     opts = {}
374: 
375:     desired_quality = [
376:       SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality,
377:       SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality,
378:     ].compact.min

380:     target_quality = @upload.target_image_quality(from, desired_quality)

385:     rescue StandardError

392:     keep_jpeg = new_size < filesize * MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO

428:     command = ["magick", from, "-auto-orient", "-background", "white", "-interlace", "none"]

430:     command << "-debug" << "all" if opts[:debug]
431:     command << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]

434:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
435:       *command,
436:       failure_message: I18n.t("upload.png_to_jpg_conversion_failure_message"),
437:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,

4: # A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
5: #
6: # Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
7: # are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
8: # when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
9: # option is present.
10: #
11: # If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
12: # secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
13: # original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
14: # post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
15: # on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
16: # complex queries and joins to perform.
17: #
18: # These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
19: # an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
20: # (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
21: # state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
22: #
23: # NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
24: # secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
25: # does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
26: # efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
27: class UploadSecurity
28:   @@custom_public_types = []
29: 
30:   PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
31:     avatar
32:     custom_emoji
33:     profile_background
34:     card_background
35:     category_logo
36:     category_logo_dark
37:     category_background
38:     group_flair
39:     badge_image
40:     site_setting
41:   ]
42: 
43:   PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
44:     Badge
45:     Category
46:     CustomEmoji
47:     Group
48:     SiteSetting
49:     ThemeField
50:     User
51:     UserAvatar
52:     UserProfile
53:   ]
54: 
55:   def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
56:     @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
57:   end
58: 
59:   # used in tests
60:   def self.reset_custom_public_types
61:     @@custom_public_types = []
62:   end
63: 
64:   def initialize(upload, opts = {})
65:     @upload = upload
66:     @opts = opts
67:     @upload_type = @opts[:type]
68:     @creating = @opts[:creating]
69:   end
70: 
71:   def should_be_secure?
72:     should_be_secure_with_reason.first
73:   end
74: 
75:   def should_be_secure_with_reason
76:     insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
77:     secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
78:       return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
79:       return true, reason if perform_check(check)
80:     end
81: 
82:     [false, "no checks satisfied"]
83:   end
84: 
85:   private
86: 
87:   def access_control_post
88:     @access_control_post ||=
89:       @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
90:   end
91: 
92:   def insecure_context_checks
93:     {
94:       secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
95:       insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
96:       public_type: "upload is public type",
97:       regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
98:       publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
99:     }
100:   end
101: 
102:   def secure_context_checks
103:     {
104:       login_required: "login is required",
105:       access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
106:       secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
107:       uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
108:       already_secure: "upload is already secure",
109:     }
110:   end

112:   # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
113:   # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
114:   # immediately if there is an access control post.
115:   def priority_check?(check)
116:     check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
117:   end

6:   def link_post_uploads(fragments: nil)
7:     upload_ids = []
8: 
9:     each_upload_url(fragments: fragments) do |url, _, sha1|
10:       upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
11: 
12:       # Link any video thumbnails
13:       if SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && upload.present? &&
14:            FileHelper.supported_video.include?(upload.extension&.downcase)
15:         # Video thumbnails have the filename of the video file sha1 with a .png or .jpg extension.
16:         # This is because at time of upload in the composer we don't know the topic/post id yet
17:         # and there is no thumbnail info added to the markdown to tie the thumbnail to the topic/post after
18:         # creation.
19:         thumbnail =
20:           Upload
21:             .where("original_filename like ?", "#{upload.sha1}.%")
22:             .order(id: :desc)
23:             .first if upload.sha1.present?
24:         if thumbnail.present?
25:           upload_ids << thumbnail.id
26:           handle_video_thumbnail(thumbnail) if respond_to?(:handle_video_thumbnail, true)
27:         end
28:       end
29:       upload_ids << upload.id if upload.present?
30:     end
31: 
32:     upload_references =
33:       upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
34:         {
35:           target_id: self.id,
36:           target_type: self.class.name,
37:           upload_id: upload_id,
38:           created_at: Time.zone.now,
39:           updated_at: Time.zone.now,
40:         }
41:       end
42: 
43:     UploadReference.transaction do
44:       UploadReference.where(target: self).delete_all
45:       UploadReference.insert_all(upload_references) if upload_references.size > 0
46: 
47:       if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
48:         Upload
49:           .where(id: upload_ids, access_control_post_id: nil)
50:           .where("id NOT IN (SELECT upload_id FROM custom_emojis)")
51:           .update_all(access_control_post_id: access_control_post_id_for_upload)
52:       end
53:     end
54:   end

56:   def each_upload_url(fragments: nil, include_local_upload: true)
57:     current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
58: 
59:     upload_patterns = [
60:       %r{/uploads/#{current_db}/},
61:       %r{/original/},
62:       %r{/optimized/},
63:       %r{/uploads/short-url/[a-zA-Z0-9]+(\.[a-z0-9]+)?},
64:     ]
65: 
66:     fragments ||= Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(self.cooked)
67: 
68:     selectors =
69:       fragments.css(
70:         "a/@href",
71:         "img/@src",
72:         "source/@src",
73:         "track/@src",
74:         "video/@poster",
75:         "div/@data-video-src",
76:         "div/@data-original-video-src",
77:       )
78: 
79:     links =
80:       selectors
81:         .map do |media|
82:           src = media.value
83:           next if src.blank?
84: 
85:           if src.end_with?("/images/transparent.png") &&
86:                (parent = media.parent)["data-orig-src"].present?
87:             parent["data-orig-src"]
88:           else
89:             src
90:           end
91:         end
92:         .compact
93:         .uniq
94: 
95:     links.each do |src|
96:       src = src.split("?")[0]
97: 
98:       if src.start_with?("upload://")
99:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(src)
100:         yield(src, nil, sha1)
101:         next
102:       end
103: 
104:       if src.include?("/uploads/short-url/")
105:         host =
106:           begin
107:             URI(src).host
108:           rescue URI::Error
109:           end
110: 
111:         next if host.present? && host != Discourse.current_hostname
112: 
113:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_path(src)
114:         yield(src, nil, sha1)
115:         next
116:       end
117: 
118:       next if upload_patterns.none? { |pattern| src =~ pattern }
119:       next if Rails.configuration.multisite && src.exclude?(current_db)
120: 
121:       src = "#{SiteSetting.force_https ? "https" : "http"}:#{src}" if src.start_with?("//")
122: 
123:       if !Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src) && !Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src) &&
124:            !(include_local_upload && src =~ %r{\A/[^/]}i)
125:         next
126:       end
127: 
128:       path =
129:         begin
130:           URI(
131:             UrlHelper.unencode(GlobalSetting.cdn_url ? src.sub(GlobalSetting.cdn_url, "") : src),
132:           )&.path
133:         rescue URI::Error
134:         end
135: 
136:       next if path.blank?
137: 
138:       sha1 =
139:         if path.include? "optimized"
140:           OptimizedImage.extract_sha1(path)
141:         else
142:           Upload.extract_sha1(path) || Upload.sha1_from_short_path(path)
